title: AI Constitution Settings
description: JSON Schema for ~/.config/aiConstitution/settings.toml. Generated from settings.toml.example. Invalid values are rejected with the specific failing constraint.

schemaVersion.description: Version of this settings schema. Matches the schemaVersion field in settings.toml. Used by 'ai update --migrate' to detect format changes.

upstream.description: Controls upstream contribution behavior — sharing hooks, skills, and findings back to convergent-systems-co/ai.

shareNewHooks.description: Share AI-authored hooks back to convergent-systems-co/ai as issues. true = file automatically without per-event prompt (pre-submission review window still shows unless skipReviewWindow=true). false = ask each time.

shareNewSkills.description: Share AI-authored skills back to the public repo. Conservative default: false. Skills can carry project-specific context that users may not want public.

shareMajorFindings.description: Auto-file major findings as 'finding' issues upstream. A 'major finding' is a violation tagged severity=major, a pattern observed >=3 times across sessions, or anything tagged with 'ai issue file --major'.

skipReviewWindow.description: Skip the per-event review window before an upstream issue is filed. Independent of shareNewHooks/shareMajorFindings — an extra opt-in layer for users who want fully automated submission.

upstreamRepo.description: Where upstream issues are filed. Format: '<owner>/<repo>'.

review.description: Memory review cadence settings. Controls how often 'ai status' and 'ai' startup nag for a memory review. Per spec §6.5.

cadenceDays.description: How often 'ai status' and 'ai' startup nag for a memory review. 0 disables the nag entirely; the codification loop still works on demand.

includeMemory.description: Whether 'ai review --check' includes memory review at this cadence.

includeAuditOverrides.description: Whether 'ai review --check' includes audit override review at this cadence.

includeAuditViolations.description: Whether 'ai review --check' includes audit violation review at this cadence.

update.description: Update reconciliation settings. Controls migration behavior on binary version bumps. Per spec §8.

autoMigratePrompt.description: On first 'ai' invocation after a binary version bump (detected via governance/last-seen-version), enter the migration prompt.

autoMigrateApprove.description: Skip per-item prompts during migration; apply all changes. Strongly discouraged — the migration prompt is designed to be walked through.

telemetry.description: Telemetry settings. Opt-in only. No other telemetry exists beyond the installPing. Adding any other key to this section is a defect.

installPing.description: Anonymous install ping (country code, OS, install method). Opt-in. No other telemetry exists.

secret_scanning.description: Pre-commit secret scanning configuration. Per spec §10.3.

installScope.description: Where to install the pre-commit secret hook. 'ai-only' — protect only the ~/.ai/ repo itself. 'current-repo' — install per-request via 'ai hooks install secret-precommit --repo=<path>'. 'all-repos' — wires into bin/clone so every fresh clone gets the hook.

allowNoVerifyBypass.description: Allow 'git commit --no-verify' to bypass the pre-commit scan. false (default) installs a ~/.ai/bin/git wrapper that strips --no-verify before exec'ing the real git. Per spec §10.3.

ciScanner.description: Whether to also wire a server-side scanner in CI as defense-in-depth. 'none' = the local pre-commit is authoritative (canonical recommendation per spec §10.3); 'gitleaks' for users who want the CI net. Trufflehog is deliberately not an option — see spec §10.4.

command_wrappers.description: Command wrapper facade configuration. Per spec §10.5.

enabled.description: Master switch. false removes the wrappers from ~/.ai/bin/; the underlying tools are reached via standard PATH.

allowDisable.description: Allow 'ai settings set command_wrappers.commands.<cmd>=disabled' at runtime. false locks wrappers on once enabled; only 'ai update --migrate' can disable.

commands.description: Per-command toggles. 'enabled' wires the wrapper; 'disabled' removes it.

git.description: Whether to install the git command wrapper.

gh.description: Whether to install the gh command wrapper.

terraform.description: Whether to install the terraform command wrapper. Opt-in; many environments don't have terraform.

kubectl.description: Whether to install the kubectl command wrapper. Opt-in.

personaRegistry.description: Where to resolve persona atoms. Both /agentic/ and /reviewer/ resolve under this base URL. Override for private mirrors that serve the same API.

profileRegistry.description: Where to resolve profile atoms. Override for private mirrors.

skillRegistry.description: Where to resolve skill atoms. NEW v0.6.

brandRegistry.description: Where to resolve brand atoms. Override for private mirrors.

actionRegistry.description: Where to resolve action atoms (atomic CI actions — composite/JS/Docker). NEW v0.10.

workflowRegistry.description: Where to resolve workflow atoms (reusable GitHub Actions workflows). v0.9; refocused v0.10.

pipelineRegistry.description: Where to resolve pipeline atoms (pipelines of workflows). NEW v0.10.

install.description: Default install behavior for the GitHub Actions trinity (action/workflow/pipeline) at setup time. Q36d in the wizard sets this.

verifyContentHash.description: Verify SHA-256 content hash on every cache load. action/workflow/pipeline atoms are verified by GitHub Actions at uses-time; this applies only when an atom is read for the catalog browser or install command.

cache.description: Per-kind cache TTL configuration. NEW v0.7, per spec §7.9.5. Cached content for a pinned version is never invalidated. TTLs govern version freshness checks and GC of unused versions.

gcRespectReferences.description: Never GC a version that's actively referenced by any profile, skill manifest, panel composition, or active mode.json — regardless of gcUnusedDays age.

agentic.description: Cache TTLs for agentic persona atoms.

freshnessCheckDays.description: How often the binary checks the registry for newer versions of agentic persona atoms.

gcUnusedDays.description: When unused/unreferenced agentic persona atom versions get GC'd.

reviewer.description: Cache TTLs for reviewer persona atoms.

freshnessCheckDays.description: How often the binary checks the registry for newer versions of reviewer persona atoms.

gcUnusedDays.description: When unused/unreferenced reviewer persona atom versions get GC'd.

freshnessCheckDays.description: How often the binary checks the registry for newer versions of profile atoms.

gcUnusedDays.description: When unused/unreferenced profile atom versions get GC'd.

freshnessCheckDays.description: How often the binary checks the registry for newer versions of skill atoms.

gcUnusedDays.description: When unused/unreferenced skill atom versions get GC'd.

freshnessCheckDays.description: How often the binary checks the registry for newer versions of brand atoms.

gcUnusedDays.description: When unused/unreferenced brand atom versions get GC'd.

action.description: Cache TTLs for action atoms. Cache governs catalog browsing and the 'ai action install' stub generator; actual content is fetched by GitHub Actions at uses-time.

freshnessCheckDays.description: How often the binary checks the registry for newer versions of action atoms.

gcUnusedDays.description: When unused/unreferenced action atom versions get GC'd.

workflow.description: Cache TTLs for workflow atoms. Cache governs catalog browsing and the 'ai workflow install' stub generator.

freshnessCheckDays.description: How often the binary checks the registry for newer versions of workflow atoms.

gcUnusedDays.description: When unused/unreferenced workflow atom versions get GC'd.

pipeline.description: Cache TTLs for pipeline atoms. Cache governs catalog browsing and the 'ai pipeline install' stub generator.

freshnessCheckDays.description: How often the binary checks the registry for newer versions of pipeline atoms.

gcUnusedDays.description: When unused/unreferenced pipeline atom versions get GC'd.

requirePinning.description: Require pinning every 'uses: convergent-systems-co/<registry>/...' to a specific version (tag or SHA), never '@main'. 'ai doctor' surfaces violations. Per spec §7.11.3 mandatory pinning is the immutability invariant.

plugins.description: Claude plugin configuration. Per spec §11. Plugins offered during wizard Q36b/c.

enabled.description: List of enabled plugin names (e.g. 'superpowers'). Plugins listed here are eligible for 'ai plugins enable'.

plannerPersonaFallback.description: Fall back to planner personas when plugins aren't installed (F12 elaboration). When true, declining a plugin during setup activates the matching agentic persona that provides degraded-but-functional guidance for the same workflow.

action.description: Action atom configuration. Atoms installed from action-atoms.com ('steps[].uses:' consumers). Per spec §7.11.

enabled.description: List of enabled action atom names (e.g. 'secret-scan-patterns', 'setup-cs-tofu', 'cs-page-deployment').

workflow.description: Workflow atom configuration. 'ai workflow install' writes .github/workflows/<name>.yml that 'uses:' the named atom. Per spec §7.11.

enabled.description: List of enabled workflow atom names (e.g. 'astro-build', 'ci-go-workspace'). The wizard (Q36f) populates this; 'ai update --migrate' re-evaluates on each upgrade.

pipeline.description: Pipeline atom configuration. Atoms installed from pipeline-atoms.com (top-level workflow composers). Q36g populates this. Per spec §7.11.

enabled.description: List of enabled pipeline atom names (e.g. 'release-train').

publishNudgeAfterDays.description: Number of days after which 'ai status' will nudge to publish an unpublished draft.

focus.description: Mode and persona focus configuration. Controls which persona/profile is loaded on session start.

defaultMode.description: Mode loaded on session start. Format: '<name>' resolves via priority order (drafts → atom registries). Use '<name>@<version>' to pin a specific atom version. 'none' means the four-file constitution only. Examples: 'polliard-coder@0.5.0' (pinned), 'polliard-coder' (latest, not recommended for stability), 'none' (no persona).

preferStableVersions.description: When resolving an unversioned name, prefer the most recent stable version over any newer pre-release. Recommended.

lastSeenWizardVersion.description: Version of governance/wizard/questions.yaml the user's answers.yaml was generated against. 'ai update --migrate' reads this to detect new questions added since the last setup.

includeSettingsFile.description: Include this settings file in 'ai sync push'. Per spec §12.4 the schema explicitly excludes secrets; nothing in settings.toml is sensitive by design. Default true; set false for per-machine settings.

paths.description: Advanced path overrides. Empty string means use the compiled-in default.

aiRoot.description: Override for the ~/.ai root directory. Empty string = use default ($HOME/.ai).

configDir.description: Override for the config directory. Empty string = use default (per os.UserConfigDir() + /aiConstitution).
